rulings.Case Title: MARIA JOYA VS. YELLOW CAB COOPERATIVE,INC., et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 28, 2009, line 5, DEFENDANT YELLOW CAB COOPERATIVE, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for Summary Adjudication is granted. Plaintiff's declaration stricken because it is not properly subscribed. (See In re Marriage of Reese & Guy (1999) 73 Cal.App.4th, 1214, 1222. Plaintiff fails to raise a triable issue of fact regarding malice. =(302/CWW/PB)

Set for hearing on Thursday, May 28, 2009, line 6, CROSS DEFENDANT DONNA SASSE, DAN ANDERSON, and, DAN ANDERSON, INC. DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Sustained, no opposition filed. Ten days leave to amend. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SEAN C MCKEAN et al VS. STEPHEN E LAWRENCE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 28, 2009, line 7, DEFENDANT SOPHIE GASPARATOS, and, STEPHEN LAWRENCE DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer is sustained in part, and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the 1st, 6th, 7th, 8th, and 9th causes of action. Plaintiffs to plead they have vacated the premises to support the 1st, 8th, and 9th causes of action. The 1st cause of action is uncertain in that it contains multiple claims for relief in violation of CRC 2.112. Plaintiffs must plead that members of the public are likely to be deceived by defendants' actions to support the 6th cause of action. The 7th cause of action is barred by the settlement agreement because it turns on facts resolved in that agreement. Demurrer overruled as to 2nd, 3rd, 4th, 5th, and 10th causes of action. =(302/CWW/JW)

rulings.Case Title: HENRY E. BARRERA VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL COR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Deft Ccsf'S Partial Demurrer To Pltf'S Complaint; P/A
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 28, 2009, line 9, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, Notice Of Hearing On Defendant City and County of San Francisco's Partial Demurrer To Plaintiff's Complaint. Sustained, no opposition filed. Ten days leave to amend. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN CHIATELLO VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 28, 2009, line 10, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff lacks standing to sue. Court may not grant injunctive relief to prevent tax collection. See Darr v. Alvord (1980) 101 Cal.App.3d 480.) Plaintiff fails to plead he is injured or will be injured by Proposition Q. Any party who may be injuried by Proposition Q. has an adequate remedy to challenge its validity by paying the tax first and then bringing a claim for refund before seeking judicial relief. No reasonable likelihood that plaintiff will be able to state a valid cause of action. =(302/CWW/JW)

rulings.Case Title: VICTORIA Y HONG VS. GREGORY CABINESS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 28, 2009, line 14, DEFENDANT GREGORY CABINESS DEMURRER TO FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer is overruled, five days leave to answer. Plaintiff alleges that after the written lease expired, the parties agreed to an oral month to month tenancy which creates a factual issue. Plaintiff alleges she complied with the San Francisco Administrative Code which is a factual issue. Demurrers for uncertainty are disfavored. Request for sanctions is denied. =(302/CWW/PB)

rulings.Case Title: INDYMAC BANK, F.S.B. VS. ESTEBAN POLONSKI et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 28, 2009, line 15, PLAINTIFF INDYMAC BANK, F.S.B. MOTION TO STRIKE. Granted without leave to amend. Cross-complaint is impermissible in an unlawful detainer proceeding. Vasey vs. California Dance Co. 70 Cal.App.3d 742,746. =(302/CWW)
